replies.msg:
As an example, we consider the heat flow in an infinite rod where the initial temperature is u(x, 0) = f(x). In other words, we look for the solution to the initial-value problem

u_t = κu_xx, -inf < x < inf, t > 0
u(x, 0) = f(x), -inf < x < inf


• STEP 1 (Transforming the problem)

Take the Fourier transform of the PDE and IC with respect to x, and get

U'(t) = -κk²U(t)
U(0) = F[f] = F(k)


• STEP 2 (Solving the transformed problem)

Solving the transformed PDE with the IC gives

U(t) = F(k)e^(-κk²t)


• STEP 3 (Finding the inverse Fourier transform)

u(x, t) = F^-1 [F(k)e^(-κk²t)] = F^-1 [F(k)] * F^-1 [e^(-κk²t)]
= f(x) * F^-1 [e^(-κk²t)]
